title: rubus rivularis subsp. spinulosus
intro: Rubus rivularis subsp. spinulosus, also known as the thorny Himalayan raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus rivularis plant, belonging to the Rosaceae family. It is a type of wild raspberry native to the Himalayan region, recognized by its thorny canes and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The thorny Himalayan raspberry has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections, and its herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from its leaves, roots, and fruits.
